frameworks.name: non-instrumental information-seeking
frameworks.one_line: people value information beyond its instrumental decision utility

frameworks.name: effort-based decision-making
frameworks.one_line: physical effort as incentive-compatible measure of information value

core_question: Do desirability and uncertainty drive willingness to exert physical effort for non-instrumental information, and does risk differ from ambiguity?
key_assumptions: physical effort is incentive-compatible measure of subjective information value
non-instrumental information has no bearing on the decision outcome
risk and ambiguity are distinct forms of uncertainty
key_findings: willingness to exert effort positively associated with outcome expected value under both risk and ambiguity
people exert more effort as outcome distribution uncertainty increases under risk
people are insensitive to ambiguity except at extreme levels
dissociation between risk and ambiguity in effort-based information-seeking
interpretations: humans engage in effort-based information-seeking for non-instrumental information under risk but not ambiguity
risk and ambiguity differentially drive the value of non-instrumental information
